title: Trump Announces Boeing Jet Order From China. Beijing Stays Silent.
summary: The deal, if it materializes, would be a major win for Boeing, which has lost ground to Airbus in one of the world’s largest aviation markets.

title: Nvidia’s Future in China Remains Unclear After Trump-Xi Summit
summary: The standoff comes as Chinese firms increasingly turn to domestic chipmakers like Huawei, in a drive to reduce China’s dependence on Western technologies.

title: Trump’s and Xi’s Body Language at the Summit Mirrored Their Styles
summary: Although at odds over issues like trade and Taiwan, the U.S. and Chinese leaders met in Beijing with a show of friendly gestures.

title: Supreme Court Allows Abortion Pill Access by Mail to Continue
summary: A federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration would have restricted access by mail to mifepristone.

title: Mail-Order Abortions Have Quickly Become Common
summary: Pills shipped to states with bans are a reason abortion has increased since Roe v. Wade was overturned.

title: House Again Blocks War Powers Vote to Halt Iran Conflict
summary: But two vulnerable Republicans joined Democrats in the effort to force President Trump to win authorization from Congress, in the latest sign of G.O.P. jitters over the war.

summary: Adm. Brad Cooper said a strike on an Iranian school may have been the only civilian casualty incident of the war.

summary: John Ratcliffe, the C.I.A. director, is the highest-ranking official in the Trump administration to visit the country.

title: Life, Death and Rebirth in Nepal, the Land of the Buddha
summary: Starting at the birthplace of Buddhism, a writer traces how its teachings spread across Asia, transforming the continent forever.

title: Mystery Grows Around Representative Thomas Kean Jr.’s Absence
summary: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. last voted in Washington on March 5, citing a medical issue. An appearance planned for late May has been canceled.

summary: What started as a taco stop with former President Barack Obama quickly turned into a very Texas debate over the proper breakfast taco order.

summary: They were shackled and sent to Kinshasa by the Trump administration. Now they face a dangerous choice: Go back to Latin America or stay in Africa.

summary: The Department of Homeland Security recently formed a unit tasked with revetting thousands of immigrants with permanent residency.

summary: Vice President JD Vance traveled to Maine for a speech, making a midterm election pitch that only Republicans could root out fraud in public benefits.

title: Why Trump’s Reflecting Pool Repairs Are in Trouble
summary: Federal records show that the no-bid contract to repair the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool and paint it blue now costs $13.1 million. That’s about seven times the amount President Trump initially said it would cost.

title: Trump-Ordered Citizenship Lists for Voting Are Likely Unreliable, Justice Dept. Says
summary: At a court hearing over a presidential order seeking to exert more control over elections, a government lawyer said no “responsible state” should rely on the lists to update their voter rolls.

title: Inside the Secret Mission to Fly Taiwan’s President to Africa
summary: From satellite phone check-ins to a borrowed royal plane, new details show how Taiwan’s leader’s team outwitted China and pulled off an audacious journey to southern Africa.

title: Enter the Killer Robots: The Ukrainian Forging the Future of Warfare
summary: Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s 35-year-old defense minister, sees futuristic military technology as crucial to his country’s survival.

title: Hantavirus Doesn’t Spread Easily, but Officials May Be Downplaying Risks
summary: The virus is clearly far less contagious than the coronavirus, scientists agree, but they have found cases where it spread among people without direct contact.

summary: They were passengers on a plane to Johannesburg with an infected Dutch woman who later died.

summary: Mahmoud Abbas’s years in power have been dogged by accusations of corruption. Many Palestinians yearn for fresh leadership.

summary: As part of a public inquiry into the massacre at Bondi Beach, dozens have described being targeted for their Jewish identity in their daily lives.

summary: Bumble is losing the feature that reshaped how we look for love. Daters may be ready for something more intentional.

summary: The decade since Brexit has been one long, painful process of trying — and failing — to make sense of this new reality.

summary: An individual with principles and quirks and an aversion to being told what to do.

title: What Are Your Evening Rituals? We Want to Know.

title: Former Oklahoma Death Row Prisoner Freed Before Retrial in a 1997 Killing
summary: A lawyer for Richard Glossip said the ruling provided a step forward in his client’s efforts to escape a “decades-long nightmare” in a case that has faced substantial criticism.

summary: Erratic behavior and staff turnover have colored Mr. Schlossberg’s bid for a House seat in New York, raising questions about his readiness for office.

title: Dallas Asks Its Residents: What Should We Do With City Hall?
summary: Faced with a billion-dollar repair bill and a desire to find a new arena for the Mavericks, the city is considering options that include demolishing the building. Residents submitted their own ideas.
